name: Wiki page naming conventions — entity primary name prefixing
description: Prevent filename collision when multiple projects/frameworks share wiki. All pages must be prefixed with entity's PRIMARY NAME (never alias). Aliases are NEVER used as file prefixes.

entities_directory.rule: Always prefix with entity's PRIMARY NAME. Never use bare concept names (e.g., messenger-component.md is WRONG, symfony-messenger.md is CORRECT).

docs_directory.rule: Always prefix with entity's PRIMARY NAME (not alias). Output name MUST match original doc filename exactly, only prepending <primary-name>.

prohibited_pattern.description: Agent MUST NOT add words beyond the prefix and original name. Adding 'system-', 'guide-', or any other modifier is a violation.

concept_prefixing_rule.rule: When creating docs/concepts related to an entity, ALWAYS use the entity's PRIMARY NAME as prefix. NEVER use aliases.
concept_prefixing_rule.rationale: Aliases are discoverability synonyms stored in frontmatter `aliases: []`. They should NOT be used for file naming because they may not match how users search (e.g., 'loom' vs 'Loomana').

examples_bad.reason: 'loom' is an alias of Loomana, NOT the primary name

comparisons_directory.rule: Compare two entities: both PRIMARY NAMES must be present in the filename.

aliases_system.primary_name_rule: Choose most common/official name as primary. Store less common variants in aliases frontmatter field.
aliases_system.rule: Aliases stored in YAML frontmatter `aliases: []` array — never create separate files for alternative names.
